title: T3 crossover uses _DIGEST_MAX instead of _AGING_MAX
description: _check_t3 computes digest_crossover_min = _DIGEST_MAX * HL (2.5×HL). For T3 to fire while STATE_AGING, age_min must simultaneously be < 1.75×HL (aging condition) AND >= 2.5×HL - lookahead. For lookahead=10 and any HL >= 13.3min this is impossible. All system tiers have HL >= 20min.
observable_symptom: T3 events = 0 in all simulation runs
confirmed_by_evidence: Run 028 trigger_threshold_analysis.md shows T3=0 for all three configs across 20 seeds

title: avg_surfaced_after is pre-collapse for push but post-collapse for poll in CSV
description: push_vs_poll_comparison.csv writes avg_fresh_at_trigger + avg_active_at_trigger for push rows (full deck count ~23.87). Poll rows come from simulate_batch_refresh which returns post-collapse avg_surfaced_after (~4.85). Same column name; different semantics.
observable_symptom: push burden appears 5× higher than poll in raw CSV; conclusion looks reversed
affected_metrics: operator_burden_csv, any analysis reading push_vs_poll_comparison.csv directly
fix_sketch: Add separate pre_collapse_items and post_collapse_items columns; apply COLLAPSE_FACTOR=0.24 before writing push rows
confirmed_by_evidence: operator_burden_comparison.md burden=440.5 for push vs 155.2 for poll — push appears worse, contradicting the recommendation

title: Re-surfaced cards not added to all_cards master list
description: check_resurface() returns new cards appended to deck via deck.extend(resurfaced). But all_cards (the master timeline) is never updated. At the next iteration deck is rebuilt from all_cards, so re-surfaced cards vanish after exactly one review cycle.
observable_symptom: total_resurfaced count is correct but the cards do not persist to subsequent reviews
affected_metrics: total_resurfaced, any analysis of re-surface persistence
fix_sketch: After deck.extend(resurfaced), also append each resurfaced card to all_cards with creation_time = current time: all_cards.extend([(float(t), c) for c in resurfaced])
confirmed_by_evidence: archive_policy_spec.md promise: re-surfaced cards cloned as fresh; lifecycle not verified in simulation

title: Recall denominator is all_records instead of all_hits
description: recall = caught / len(records). len(records) = total cards including EXPIRED ones (never hit). True recall = caught / n_hits. Comparing precision (caught / non_expired) against recall (caught / all_cards) is meaningless; recall is systematically underestimated by the fraction of expired cards.
observable_symptom: Recall values appear ~2× lower than actual for tiers with ~50% hit rates
affected_metrics: recall in HL calibration scenario comparison, HL recommendation verdicts
fix_sketch: Change denominator to n_hits: n_hits = sum(1 for r in records if r['outcome_result'] in (OUTCOME_HIT, OUTCOME_PARTIAL)); recall = caught / max(n_hits, 1)
confirmed_by_evidence: Recall of 0.0 for HL < calibrated value would be suspicious — not yet cross-checked against run artifacts

title: S2 suppresses T3-triggered pushes when no fresh/active cards exist
description: _check_suppress_s2 returns True immediately when fresh_active is empty. If T3 fires (aging card about to expire) but no fresh/active cards are present, S2 overrides the T3 trigger. The aging card — which was the entire reason for T3 — is silently suppressed.
observable_symptom: T3 pushes for aging-only decks are suppressed; operator misses last-chance aging cards
affected_metrics: t3_events (secondary — currently zero due to BUG-001), missed_critical_count
fix_sketch: Before returning True, check if triggers includes 'T3': if 'T3' in (triggers passed in or evaluated upstream) then return False. Better: pass triggers into _check_suppress_s2 and exempt T3 events from S2.
confirmed_by_evidence: Latent until BUG-001 is fixed; will manifest immediately after T3 crossover is corrected

title: STATE_AGING counted in stale_states despite being in _SURFACED_STATES
description: stale_states = {STATE_AGING, STATE_DIGEST_ONLY, STATE_EXPIRED}. But _SURFACED_STATES = frozenset([STATE_FRESH, STATE_ACTIVE, STATE_AGING]). Aging cards ARE shown to operator at full detail (last-chance review), yet they inflate stale_rate. This makes stale_rate a blend of 'degraded' and 'at risk'.
observable_symptom: stale_rate is inflated by ~10-30% depending on HL distribution; 45min stale=0.21 includes aging cards
affected_metrics: stale_rate, all stale_rate comparisons across runs 026-028
fix_sketch: Either remove STATE_AGING from stale_states (redefine stale as cards NOT shown in full review), or add a separate 'at_risk_rate' for aging cards. Document the choice explicitly.
confirmed_by_evidence: _SURFACED_STATES vs stale_states overlap is visible in code; no artifact directly contradicts this but cadence conclusions may be directionally affected

title: expiry_before_hit and decayed_but_late_hit are unconditionally aliased
description: decayed_but_late_hit = expiry_before_hit with comment 'same concept in this dataset'. These are semantically distinct: expiry_before_hit = hits where TTE > current_HL (would be missed); decayed_but_late_hit = hits that arrived after decay started. The alias hides whether they are actually the same.
observable_symptom: HL calibration reports conflate two distinct failure modes
fix_sketch: Compute each independently. expiry_before_hit: TTE > current_HL. decayed_but_late_hit: TTE > active_window (time card is in fresh+active states).
confirmed_by_evidence: Code comment explicitly acknowledges the alias: 'same concept in this dataset' — context-dependent correctness

title: rank_delta sign is inverted relative to 'demoted' semantics
description: rank_delta = raw_rank - uplift_aware_rank. If raw_rank=5 and uplift_aware_rank=2, delta=3 (positive) but the card IMPROVED (moved up, lower rank number = better). The code then sorts 'demoted' cards by rank_delta ascending, which lists the most-improved cards first — opposite of intent.
observable_symptom: Demotion report lists improved cards; promotion report may list demoted cards
fix_sketch: Invert: rank_delta = uplift_aware_rank - raw_rank, so negative = improvement (rescued), positive = demotion. Or swap sort order for demotion list.
confirmed_by_evidence: Run artifacts from uplift_ranker not directly cited in top-level conclusions; medium risk

title: symmetric_difference / 2 for top-k change count assumes equal-size sets
description: n_top_k_changed = len(sym_diff) // 2. This is correct only if |top_k| is fixed and exactly one card exits for each that enters. If top_k sizes differ between raw and normalized ranking (e.g., tie-breaks), sym_diff length can be odd, and // 2 underestimates changes by 0.5.
observable_symptom: n_top_k_changed is off by 0 or 1 in edge cases
fix_sketch: Use len(raw_top_k_ids - ua_top_k_ids) (cards that exited) as the canonical count; this is always unambiguous.

title: Prune threshold inconsistency between batch_refresh and push_surfacing
description: simulate_batch_refresh prunes cards at 3.0×HL; simulate_push_surfacing prunes at 5.0×HL (_ARCHIVE_RATIO). The archive threshold is 5.0×HL. Cards in the 3.0–5.0×HL range would be pruned before archive in batch_refresh but survive in push_surfacing. These functions are used for different runs so impact is contained.
observable_symptom: Archive metrics would differ between the two simulation paths if archive were added to batch_refresh
affected_metrics: avg_archived, any archive comparison between simulation modes
fix_sketch: Unify prune threshold to archive_max_age_min (480 min) in both functions, as done in simulate_batch_refresh_with_archive.
confirmed_by_evidence: Contained risk: batch_refresh does not use archives; batch_refresh_with_archive uses 480min already

title: EMA alpha naming convention is reversed from industry standard
description: _EMA_ALPHA = 0.40 is applied as: ema = 0.40 * new_value + 0.60 * prev_ema. In most EMA implementations, higher alpha = more weight on recent value. Here alpha=0.40 means 40% weight on new — typical naming would use alpha for the decay factor not the new-value weight.
observable_symptom: EMA smoothing behaves correctly but naming may confuse future maintainers; no functional impact
fix_sketch: Rename _EMA_ALPHA to _EMA_SMOOTHING_FACTOR and update comment: 'fraction of weight on new observation'.
